extracted_json.title: Step 3 of the SPSP: Execute the plan — Structural map (PC Industry, 1980s)

blocks.text: Step 3 of the SPSP : Execute the plan

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Step 3 of the SPSP: building a structural (Five Forces) map of the PC industry in the 1980s.
extracted_json.summary_gist: This page covers Step 3 of the SPSP (Execute the plan), specifically Step 1: creating a structural map, illustrated with the Personal Computer Industry of the 1980s. The diagram maps the competitive forces around central Competitors (F1: Rivalry between Competitors), including New entrants (Compaq, NCR) with F2 threat of new entrants and barriers to entry; Suppliers (microprocessors—Intel, AMD; operating systems—Microsoft; storage—Seagate) with F4 supplier power; Buyers (consumers, enterprises) with F5 buyer power; Substitutes (pen computing—GO, Apple; PDA—Palm) with F3 threat of substitutes; Complementors (Intel) with F6 power to influence; and the existing PC competitors (IBM, HP, Dell, Apple).

transcription:
